Home / Services / Professional Tree Pruning

Professional Tree Pruning

Health-focused pruning for Charlotte's signature oaks, magnolias, and crepe myrtles.

Proper pruning extends the life of Charlotte's trees while maintaining their natural beauty. Our techniques vary for different species - from the crepe myrtles in NoDa to the heritage oaks in Myers Park.

We follow ANSI standards while accounting for local factors like frequent summer thunderstorms that can exploit poor cuts. Crown thinning improves airflow to reduce disease in our humid climate, while careful reduction pruning helps young trees in Fort Mill developments withstand high winds.

Seasonal timing matters in our region - we avoid heavy pruning during active beetle seasons and schedule oak work for winter months to prevent disease transmission.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Species-specific health assessment
  2. 02 Step 2: Structural pruning plan development
  3. 03 Step 3: Precision cutting with sterilized tools
  4. 04 Step 4: Nutrient application for stressed trees

Benefits

  • Reduces storm damage risk
  • Improves flowering for ornamentals
  • Prevents disease spread
  • Maintains property sight lines

Frequently Asked Questions

When should I prune my Charlotte trees?

Most pruning occurs in winter, but timing varies by species - we'll create a schedule tailored to your landscape.

How much should be removed at once?

We never remove more than 25% of canopy to avoid stressing trees in our hot summers.

Get a Free Estimate

Ready to get started with professional tree pruning? Contact us today.

Request Quote Or call: 555-tre-r90
